she created [...] for her heart.

o ii 3'3'

o ii 4'4'

[...] she threw down in the open country.

r i 1'r 1'

⸢ut⸣-[ta-as-si-iḫ ...]

(the hunter said to Gilgameš:) 'he (Enkidu) has uprooted [...].

r i 2'2'

uš-⸢te⸣-[li ...]

'He has removed [...],

r i 3'3'

ul i-[nam-din-an-ni ...]

'he will not give me over [...].'

r i 4'4'

dGIŠ-⸢gim₂⸣-[maš ...]

r i 5'5'

a-lik ṣa-[a-a-du ...]

Column ii
(start of column missing)
r ii 1'r 1'

[...] x x [... ta-rap]-pu-ud [x]

(Šamhat said to Enkidu:) '[...] ... [...] do you (Enkidu) roam [...]?

r ii 2'2'

[x x lu-tar]-⸢ri⸣-ka ana ⸢ŠA₃ UNUG⸣ki su-pur

'[...] let me guide you to Uruk the sheepfold,

r ii 3'3'

[...] ⸢mu⸣-ša₂-bu ša₂ da-nu-um

'[...], the dwelling of Anu,

r ii 4'4'

[...]-ma ana ŠA₃ UNUGki su-pur

'[...] ... to Uruk the sheepfold,

r ii 5'5'

[x E₂.AN].⸢NA⸣ qud-du-šu₂ mu-ša₂-bu ša₂ d15

'[...] purified Eana, the dwelling of Ištar,

r ii 6'6'

[x x dGIŠ]-⸢gim₂⸣-maš git₂-ma-lu e-mu-qam

'[...] Gilgameš is perfect in strength,

r ii 7'7'

[... ug]-da-aš₂-ša₂-ri UGU GURUŠ-MEŠ

'[...] dominates over the young men!'

r ii 8'8'

[i-ta-ma-aš₃]-⸢šum⸣-ma ma-gir qa-ba-a-[ša₂]

She spoke to him and what she said was agreeable,

r ii 9'9'

[...] ⸢lib₃⸣-ba-šu₂-ma i-še-ʾa-a ⸢ib⸣-[ra]

[...] his heart was looking for a friend.

r ii 10'10'

[... a]-⸢na⸣ ša₂-ši-ma MU⸢ar₂ ana munus⸣[ḫa-rim-ti]

[...] he (Enkidu) spoke to her, to the prostitute:

r ii 11'11'

[x x f]⸢šam⸣-ḫat ⸢qi₂-ri⸣-[in-ni ...]

'[...], O Šamhat, invite me

r ii 12'12'

[...] ⸢qud⸣-[du-ši ...]

'[...] purified [...]'
